channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Alibaba May Launch Enterprise AI Agent This Week
item.description: Alibaba is poised to launch a new enterprise-grade AI agent application as early as this week, developed by its workplace collaboration platform DingTalk, signaling a major push into the B2B AI market.

item.title: Alibaba Launches New AI Division “Alibaba Token Hub,” Led by CEO Eddie Wu
item.description: Alibaba CEO Eddie Wu is directly taking the reins of the newly formed Alibaba Token Hub (ATH) business group, a major restructuring aimed at unifying the company's AI efforts around the creation, distribution, and application of tokens.

item.title: SmartMore Files for Hong Kong IPO With USD 150 million Annual Revenue
item.description: AI pioneer Dr. Jia Jiaya's SmartMore has filed for a Hong Kong IPO, aiming to become the "first industrial AI agent" publicly listed company, with 2025 revenues reaching USD 150 million.

item.title: Horizon Robotics Chip Head Chen Peng Reportedly Set to Depart
item.description: Horizon Robotics' head of chip R&D, Chen Peng, is reportedly leaving the company at a critical juncture as it prepares its next-generation Journey 7 chip to compete in an increasingly crowded market.

item.title: Ropedia Raises Tens of Millions in Seed Funding to Build Robotics Data Infrastructure
item.description: Singapore-based robotics data infrastructure startup Ropedia has secured tens of millions of dollars in seed funding from investors associated with Google, NVIDIA, and Amazon to reduce the cost of robot training data by 50x.

item.title: INC0153706
item.description: Mar 16, 23:45 UTCIdentified - Current status: We've determined a recent improvement to Snowflake alerts creation and alteration contains a code issue, which is causing the impact. We've developed a fix and are rolling it out to restore service. We'll provide another update within 6 hours.Customer...

item.title: Elevated R2 Error Rate in India
item.description: Mar 16, 22:48 UTCResolved - We were able to identify and apply an effective fix for this issue. This incident is now resolved.Mar 16, 21:56 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue where customers accessing R2 from India may see high error rates and increased latency for their R2...

item.title: The First Healthcare Robotics Dataset and Foundational Physical AI Models for Healthcare Robotics
GlassWorm Attack Uses Stolen GitHub Tokens to Force-Push Malware Into Python Repos
item.description: The GlassWorm malware campaign is being used to fuel an ongoing attack that leverages the stolen GitHub tokens to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories. "The attack targets Python projects — including Django apps, ML research code, Streamlit dashboards, and PyPI packages — by...

item.title: Cloudflare Analytics Availability Issues
item.description: Mar 16, 18:55 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating issues with Cloudflare Analytics. Customers might experience elevated rate of errors, increased latency while reading data via Cloudflare API, and/or delays in updating analytics data.

item.title: Where OpenAI’s technology could show up in Iran
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. It’s been just over two weeks since OpenAI reached a controversial agreement to allow the Pentagon to use its AI in classified environments. There are...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 16, 16:35 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 16, 15:35 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. This is currently only impacting free Claude.ai users.Mar 16, 15:13 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Mar 16, 14:05 UTCUpdate - We are...

item.title: Oracle EBS Hack: Only 4 Corporate Giants Still Silent on Potential Impact
item.description: Broadcom, Bechtel, Estée Lauder, and Abbott Technologies are the only major companies that have yet to issue a public statement. The post Oracle EBS Hack: Only 4 Corporate Giants Still Silent on Potential Impact appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cloudflare Zero Trust: Gateway Issues
item.description: Mar 16, 15:42 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with intermittent failures in the API for Zero Trust Gateway.Customers may be unable to view or edit their policies in the Zero Trust dashboard. Existing policies are not impacted.More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Elevated Error Rates for Workers AI Services
item.description: Mar 16, 15:35 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 16, 15:31 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 16, 13:58 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an elevated rate of errors, specifically an increase in timeout (408) errors,...

item.title: Errors starting and connecting to Codespaces
item.description: Mar 16, 15:28 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 16, 15:27 UTCUpdate - Errors starting or resuming Codespaces have resolved.Mar 16,...

item.title: Security Firm Executive Targeted in Sophisticated Phishing Attack
item.description: The attackers used a DKIM-signed phishing email, trusted redirect infrastructure, compromised servers, and Cloudflare-protected phishing pages. The post Security Firm Executive Targeted in Sophisticated Phishing Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: Mar 16, 14:21 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 16, 13:56 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Days, Router Botnets, AWS Breach, Rogue AI Agents & More
item.description: Some weeks in security feel normal. Then you read a few tabs and get that immediate “ah, great, we’re doing this now” feeling. This week has that energy. Fresh messes, old problems getting sharper, and research that stops feeling theoretical real fast. A few bits hit a little too close to real...

item.title: The next phase of open models
item.description: Markets, capabilities, cope, and bewilderment in the industrialization of language models.

item.title: Nurturing agentic AI beyond the toddler stage
item.description: Parents of young children face a lot of fears about developmental milestones, from infancy through adulthood. The number of months it takes a baby to learn to talk or walk is often used as a benchmark for wellness, or an indicator of additional tests needed to properly diagnose a potential health...

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Hit Asian Militaries in Patient Espionage Operation
item.description: The state-sponsored hackers deployed custom tools and stayed dormant in the compromised environments for months. The post China-Linked Hackers Hit Asian Militaries in Patient Espionage Operation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: Mar 16, 12:36 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 16, 12:27 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 16, 12:01 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: The Download: glass chips and “AI-free” logos
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Future AI chips could be built on glass  Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI...

item.title: Threat Actor Targeting VPN Users in New Credential Theft Campaign
item.description: Storm-2561 is distributing fake VPN clients through SEO poisoning, deploying trojans, and stealing login information. The post Threat Actor Targeting VPN Users in New Credential Theft Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Why Security Validation Is Becoming Agentic
item.description: If you run security at any reasonably complex organization, your validation stack probably looks something like this: a BAS tool in one corner. A pentest engagement, or maybe an automated pentesting product, in another. A vulnerability scanner feeding an attack surface management platform somewhere...

item.title: ForceMemo: Python Repositories Compromised in GlassWorm Aftermath
item.description: Hundreds of GitHub accounts were accessed using credentials stolen in the VS Code GlassWorm campaign. The post ForceMemo: Python Repositories Compromised in GlassWorm Aftermath appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Spread MacSync macOS Infostealer via Fake AI Tool Installers
item.description: Three different ClickFix campaigns have been found to act as a delivery vector for the deployment of a macOS information stealer called MacSync. "Unlike traditional exploit-based attacks, this method relies entirely on user interaction – usually in the form of copying and executing commands –...

item.title: Securing digital assets against future threats
DRILLAPP Backdoor Targets Ukraine, Abuses Microsoft Edge Debugging for Stealth Espionage
item.description: Ukrainian entities have emerged as the target of a new campaign likely orchestrated by threat actors linked to Russia, according to a report from S2 Grupo's LAB52 threat intelligence team. The campaign, observed in February 2026, has been assessed to share overlaps with a prior campaign mounted by...

item.title: Context-Enriched Natural Language Descriptions of Vessel Trajectories
item.description: arXiv:2603.12287v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We address the problem of transforming raw vessel trajectory data collected from AIS into structured and semantically enriched representations interpretable by humans and directly usable by machine reasoning systems. We propose a context-aware...

item.title: Efficient Reasoning with Balanced Thinking
item.description: arXiv:2603.12372v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) have shown remarkable reasoning capabilities, yet they often suffer from overthinking, expending redundant computational steps on simple problems, or underthinking, failing to explore sufficient reasoning paths despite...

item.title: Generating Expressive and Customizable Evals for Timeseries Data Analysis Agents with AgentFuel
item.description: arXiv:2603.12483v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Across many domains (e.g., IoT, observability, telecommunications, cybersecurity), there is an emerging adoption of conversational data analysis agents that enable users to "talk to your data" to extract insights. Such data analysis agents operate on...

item.title: AI Planning Framework for LLM-Based Web Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.12710v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Developing autonomous agents for web-based tasks is a core challenge in AI. While Large Language Model (LLM) agents can interpret complex user requests, they often operate as black boxes, making it difficult to diagnose why they fail or how they plan....

item.title: On Using Machine Learning to Early Detect Catastrophic Failures in Marine Diesel Engines
item.description: arXiv:2603.12733v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Catastrophic failures of marine engines imply severe loss of functionality and destroy or damage the systems irreversibly. Being sudden and often unpredictable events, they pose a severe threat to navigation, crew, and passengers. The abrupt nature...

item.title: No More DeLuLu: Physics-Inspired Kernel Networks for Geometrically-Grounded Neural Computation
item.description: arXiv:2603.12276v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the yat-product, a kernel operator combining quadratic alignment with inverse-square proximity. We prove it is a Mercer kernel, analytic, Lipschitz on bounded domains, and self-regularizing, admitting a unique RKHS embedding. Neural...

item.title: From Garbage to Gold: A Data-Architectural Theory of Predictive Robustness
item.description: arXiv:2603.12288v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tabular machine learning presents a paradox: modern models achieve state-of-the-art performance using high-dimensional (high-D), collinear, error-prone data, defying the "Garbage In, Garbage Out" mantra. To help resolve this, we synthesize principles...

item.title: Multi-objective Genetic Programming with Multi-view Multi-level Feature for Enhanced Protein Secondary Structure Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2603.12293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predicting protein secondary structure is essential for understanding protein function and advancing drug discovery. However, the intricate sequence-structure relationship poses significant challenges for accurate modeling. To address these, we...

item.title: Synthetic Data Generation for Brain-Computer Interfaces: Overview, Benchmarking, and Future Directions
item.description: arXiv:2603.12296v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning has achieved transformative performance across diverse domains, largely driven by the large-scale, high-quality training data. In contrast, the development of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) is fundamentally constrained by the limited,...

item.title: Global Evolutionary Steering: Refining Activation Steering Control via Cross-Layer Consistency
item.description: arXiv:2603.12298v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation engineering enables precise control over Large Language Models (LLMs) without the computational cost of fine-tuning. However, existing methods deriving vectors from static activation differences are susceptible to high-dimensional noise and...

item.title: Task-Specific Knowledge Distillation via Intermediate Probes
item.description: arXiv:2603.12270v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge distillation from large language models (LLMs) assumes that the teacher's output distribution is a high-quality training signal. On reasoning tasks, this assumption is frequently violated. A model's intermediate representations may encode...

item.title: Diagnosing Retrieval Bias Under Multiple In-Context Knowledge Updates in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.12271v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are widely used in knowledge-intensive tasks where the same fact may be revised multiple times within context. Unlike prior work focusing on one-shot updates or single conflicts, multi-update scenarios contain multiple historically valid versions...

item.title: ActTail: Global Activation Sparsity in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.12272v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation sparsity is a promising approach for accelerating large language model (LLM) inference by reducing computation and memory movement. However, existing activation sparsity methods typically apply uniform sparsity across projections, ignoring...

item.title: Aligning Language Models from User Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2603.12273v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-turn user interactions are among the most abundant data produced by language models, yet we lack effective methods to learn from them. While typically discarded, these interactions often contain useful information: follow-up user messages may...

item.title: GONE: Structural Knowledge Unlearning via Neighborhood-Expanded Distribution Shaping
item.description: arXiv:2603.12275v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Unlearning knowledge is a pressing and challenging task in Large Language Models (LLMs) because of their unprecedented capability to memorize and digest training data at scale, raising more significant issues regarding safety, privacy, and...

item.title: SSL Certificate Provisioning Delays
item.description: Mar 15, 23:10 UTCResolved - This incident is now resolved.Mar 15, 22:50 UTCMonitoring - We have identified and implemented a fix for this issue. We are currently monitoring the results.Mar 15, 22:46 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating delays in provisioning SSL certificates. New SSL...

item.title: [AINews] Context Drought
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on Anthropic's belated GA of 1M context windows after Gemini and OpenAI.

item.title: Responses API errors when using background mode
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Responses (Operational)

item.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Introducing NVIDIA NeMo Retriever’s Generalizable Agentic Retrieval Pipeline
Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 13, 18:37 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.
